meaning: n.畏缩,后退,弹回,反作用,后座力vi.退却,畏缩,弹回,撤退,反冲
examples: People used to recoil from the idea of getting into debt.
An evil deed may recoil on the head of the doer.
They find it unpleasant and they recoil from it.
Younger voters who lean libertarian recoil from both the budget and the vision.
But most people rightly recoil when asked to comment on this approach for science and medicine.
